# Evidence Ledger E6 — Training Data & Multimodal Alignment
<!-- auto-generated by evidence-collector; do not hand-edit -->

---
## Q1 — LLaVA visual-instruction-tuning recipe

### LLaVA two-stage training procedure
- **value/result**: Stage 1 (feature-alignment pretrain): visual encoder + LLM frozen; only the projection matrix W is trained; trained on 595K CC3M image-text pairs (filtered from full CC3M) for 1 epoch. Stage 2 (end-to-end instruction finetuning): projection + LLM both updated; trained on 158K GPT-4-generated instruction-following samples for 3 epochs.
- **condition**: 8×A100; LR 2e-3 (Stage 1), 2e-5 (Stage 2); batch 128 / 32
- **source**: Liu, Li, Wu, Lee — "Visual Instruction Tuning" (LLaVA), NeurIPS 2023, arXiv:2304.08485v2 · **tier**: A · **confidence**: high

### LLaVA GPT-4-generated instruction data
- **value/result**: 158K unique language-image instruction-following samples generated by language-only GPT-4 from COCO image captions and bounding-box annotations (no raw image passed to GPT-4). Three types: 58K conversation, 23K detailed description, 77K complex reasoning. ~80K unique images from COCO.
- **condition**: COCO images; GPT-4 given symbolic representations (captions + bounding boxes) as text context
- **source**: Liu et al., arXiv:2304.08485v2 (pp. 3–5 of PDF) · **tier**: A · **confidence**: high

### LLaVA-1.5 improvements — data & architecture
- **value/result**: Replaces linear projection with MLP; uses CLIP-ViT-L-336px. Pretraining unchanged (~600K CC3M subset). Instruction-tuning expanded to 1.2M total samples mixing LLaVA-Instruct-158K + VQA-v2, OKVQA, A-OKVQA, OCRVQA, TextCaps, Visual Genome, RefCOCO, GQA, ShareGPT (~150K). Achieves SoTA on 11 benchmarks with a 13B checkpoint trained in ~1 day on 8×A100.
- **condition**: LLaVA-1.5 7B and 13B; CLIP-ViT-L-14 336px; Vicuna-1.5
- **source**: Liu, Li, Li, Lee — "Improved Baselines with Visual Instruction Tuning" (LLaVA-1.5), arXiv:2310.03744v2 (ECCV 2024) · **tier**: A · **confidence**: high

---
## Q2 — Pretraining data sources for VLMs

### LAION-5B dataset
- **value/result**: 5.85 billion CLIP-filtered image-text pairs crawled from CommonCrawl with alt-text. Filtered by CLIP cosine similarity (retaining ~10% of raw pairs). Subsets: 2.32B English, 2.26B multilingual, 1.27B no-language. First open dataset at this scale; used to train CLIP, Stable Diffusion, and many VLMs.
- **condition**: CLIP ViT-B/32 and L/14 used for filtering; web-scale, no single domain restriction
- **source**: Schuhmann et al. — "LAION-5B: An Open Large-Scale Dataset for Training Next Generation Image-Text Models", NeurIPS 2022 Datasets & Benchmarks, arXiv:2210.08402 · **tier**: A · **confidence**: high

### COYO-700M dataset
- **value/result**: ~700M image-alt-text pairs curated from CommonCrawl, emphasizing informative image-text correlation. Applies stricter quality filtering than LAION (CLIP score threshold, face-blurring option, NSFW removal, watermark detection). Used as a complementary pretraining source to LAION for several VLMs.
- **condition**: Web-crawled; additional safety and quality filters applied
- **source**: Secondary search results (no primary paper arXiv ID confirmed from search); referenced in multiple VLM survey papers as "COYO-700M (Kakaobrain 2022)" · **tier**: B · **confidence**: med

### MMC4 (Multimodal C4) dataset
- **value/result**: 101.2M documents with 571M images interleaved in 43B English tokens, built from the cleaned C4 corpus. Images are inserted into text at sentence level via CLIP-based zero-shot bipartite assignment (CLIP ViT-L/14). Enables in-context few-shot learning for multimodal models. Used to train OpenFlamingo. Subsets available: fewer-faces and "core" (stricter filtering).
- **condition**: Derived from C4 (English web text); CLIP ViT-L/14 for image-sentence alignment; NeurIPS 2023
- **source**: Zhu et al. — "Multimodal C4: An Open, Billion-scale Corpus of Images Interleaved with Text", NeurIPS 2023, arXiv:2304.06939 · **tier**: A · **confidence**: high

### ShareGPT4V dataset
- **value/result**: 1.2M image-text pairs with highly detailed captions generated by GPT-4V. Initial 100K captions generated directly by GPT-4V across diverse image sources (COCO, LAION, Conceptual Captions, SAM, TextCaps, WikiArt). Remaining 1.1M generated by a trained Share-Captioner model. Mean caption length ~942 chars (100K subset) vs. ~826 chars (1.2M). Used as pretraining data for alignment stage in several VLMs; improves semantic grounding compared to short alt-text.
- **condition**: Two-stage training: 1.2M pairs for pretraining alignment; then visual instruction tuning on top. ECCV 2024.
- **source**: Chen et al. — "ShareGPT4V: Improving Large Multi-Modal Models with Better Captions", ECCV 2024, arXiv:2311.12793 · **tier**: A · **confidence**: high

---
## Q3 — Multimodal alignment / hallucination mitigation

### RLHF-V — fine-grained correctional human feedback
- **value/result**: Collects 1.4K human-annotated fine-grained corrections of hallucinated segments in MLLM responses (object, position, number, attribute, action hallucinations). Uses dense segment-level DPO training. Reduces object hallucination by 34.8% on MHumanEval and by 75.8% on Object HalBench (relative) compared to the Muffin base model. Outperforms the concurrent LLaVA-RLHF trained on 10K samples.
- **condition**: Base model: Muffin; 1.4K annotated samples; 1 hour on 8×A100; CVPR 2024
- **source**: Yu et al. — "RLHF-V: Towards Trustworthy MLLMs via Behavior Alignment from Fine-grained Correctional Human Feedback", CVPR 2024, arXiv:2312.00849 · **tier**: A · **confidence**: high

### RLAIF-V — AI feedback for multimodal alignment
- **value/result**: Replaces human feedback with open-source AI model feedback using deconfounded sampling and divide-and-conquer judgment to improve feedback quality and avoid bias. Claims SoTA on both generative and discriminative trustworthiness benchmarks, surpassing GPT-4V-level trustworthiness. Scales to any MLLM without human annotation overhead.
- **condition**: Open-source feedback models; evaluated vs. GPT-4V baseline; CVPR 2025
- **source**: Yu et al. — "RLAIF-V: Open-Source AI Feedback Leads to Super GPT-4V Trustworthiness", CVPR 2025, arXiv:2405.17220 · **tier**: A · **confidence**: high

### POVID — preference optimization via noise injection
- **value/result**: Frames VLM hallucination as a modality misalignment problem. Creates dispreferred responses via two strategies: (1) GPT-4V injects hallucinated text into ground-truth preferred responses; (2) diffusion noise added to image to elicit the model's inherent hallucination behavior. Both strategies combined in a modified DPO loss for preference fine-tuning.
- **condition**: VLLMs; DPO-style training; arXiv Feb 2024
- **source**: Zhou et al. — "Aligning Modalities in Vision Large Language Models via Preference Fine-tuning" (POVID), arXiv:2402.11411 (Feb 2024) · **tier**: B · **confidence**: high

### mDPO — conditional preference optimization for multimodal LLMs
- **value/result**: Identifies "unconditional preference" problem in standard DPO applied to multimodal models: the model learns language-only preferences and ignores the image condition, worsening hallucination. mDPO adds a conditional image preference objective to anchor optimization on visual content. Claims up to 38% improvement over prior SOTA on standard hallucination benchmarks; consistently reduces hallucination across model sizes.
- **condition**: Multiple model sizes; evaluated on 3 hallucination benchmarks; EMNLP 2024
- **source**: Wang et al. — "mDPO: Conditional Preference Optimization for Multimodal Large Language Models", EMNLP 2024, arXiv:2406.11839 · **tier**: A · **confidence**: high
